confirmed.title: complete_json span missing parse attempts on failure path
confirmed.verification: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/src/reasoning_client.py:669 sets json.parse_attempts ONLY on success path when _extract_json() succeeds. Lines 675-677 (failure path after loop exhaustion when all MAX_RETRIES+1 attempts fail) do NOT set this attribute. Reachable via test_complete_json_exhausts_retries_then_raises (line 167 of test_reasoning_client.py). Parallel to complete() function (lines 562 vs 586) which DOES set llm.retries symmetrically on both paths. NOT on WONTFIX list (HARDENING_LOG.md lines 289-303).
confirmed.recommended_action: Set json.parse_attempts attribute on complete_json failure path: after line 674, before record_exception() on line 675, add span.set_attribute('json.parse_attempts', attempt + 1) where attempt is MAX_RETRIES (loop variable's final value). This mirrors the success-path pattern (line 669) and the complete() function's symmetric retry recording (lines 562 and 586), ensuring operators can determine parse attempt count from the span regardless of success/failure outcome.
confirmed.proposed_fix: Set a 'json.parse_attempts' attribute on the complete_json span in the failure path as well. After line 674 (after the loop exits), add: `span.set_attribute('json.parse_attempts', attempt + 1)` before record_exception(). This mirrors the logic on line 669 (success path) and the complete() pattern of recording retries on both paths.

confirmed.title: PostgreSQL connection string leaked in error messages
confirmed.verification:
Verified via code inspection:
- /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/src/checkpointer.py:207 directly interpolates conn_str into RuntimeError message
- /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/src/checkpointer.py:82 documents "Never logged, never committed" contract
- /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/src/infrastructure/config.py:47-60 require_local_uri() only validates hostname; does not strip credentials
- /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/docs/architecture/AGENT_WORKFLOW.md shows credentials IN URIs are standard (hcgrc_agent:***@localhost)
- /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/tests/test_phase0/test_postgres_checkpointer.py:113-124 tests confirm error path is reachable and real
- No sanitization, masking, or redaction present in checkpointer.py error handling
- Not in WONTFIX list in HARDENING_LOG.md
confirmed.recommended_action: Extract hostname and port from the parsed URI using urlparse, then construct a sanitized error message like: f"PostgresSaver connection failed. Is Postgres running on {host}:{port}? Error: {e}" without including the full conn_str. This preserves the helpful diagnostic information (host:port) while preventing credential exposure, aligning with the ADR-0002 contract documented at line 82.
confirmed.proposed_fix: Sanitize the connection string before including in error messages. Extract only the loopback-safe components (host:port/database) without credentials: Extract hostname and port from the parsed URI and construct a sanitized message like f'PostgresSaver connection failed. Is Postgres running on {host}:{port}?'. Do not include the full conn_str variable.
